title: Anonymous Forward (Forward Without Source Attribution)
description: Allow a user to forward a message without revealing the original sender identity. The forwarded copy appears as if the forwarder authored it directly — no 'Forwarded from X' header. This is a privacy-preserving relay mechanism, not anonymization of the relay actor.

security_model.threat: Adversary uses anonymous forward to strip accountability from harmful/illegal content, enabling disinformation laundering.
mitigations: Server-side: original_message_id and original_sender_id MUST be stored in a non-public shadow table (forward_provenance) — not exposed to recipients but available to trust & safety.
Trust & Safety retains full forward chain for CSAM/NCII/terrorism content under legal hold (immutable audit log).
Rate limit anonymous forwards per user_id: max 20/hour / 100/day via token bucket enforced at API gateway.
Zero-trust: client cannot assert 'anonymous=true' without server-side re-validation of user's current permission level (not banned, account age ≥ N days).
Replay protection: idempotency_key per forward request; duplicate attempts within 30s window rejected (HTTP 409).

forward_provenance_table.rls: SELECT restricted to service_role and trust_safety_role ONLY. No user-facing RLS SELECT policy.

server_enforced_rules: If anonymous=true: verify account_age ≥ 30 days, user not in restricted_users list.
Strip original_sender metadata from message payload sent to recipients.
Write provenance row regardless of anonymous flag.
Return 403 if original message's owner set allow_anonymous_forward=false (future: per-message privacy setting).

attribution_display.anonymous_forward: No header — message appears as original content from forwarder

failure_handling.partial_failure: If forward succeeds to 3/5 selected conversations, return 207 Multi-Status with per-item results.
failure_handling.rollback: Each forward is an independent DB write — no cross-conversation transaction. Failed items do not affect succeeded ones.
